sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Akari Therapeutics 10-K: $(17.3)M Net Loss, R&D Cuts and ADC Development Focus
feed.summary: Akari Therapeutics reported a net loss of $(17.3) million and operating losses of $(17.3) million, a 20% reduction from the prior year due to lower merger-related and RIF costs. The company significantly cut R&D expenses by 60% to $2.815 million, focusing on its prioritized ADC program, AKTX-101, with a Phase 1 trial targeted for Q1 2027. Akari has shifted its strategic focus to a PH1-based ADC pipeline following a merger and secured multiple financings to support its development.

feed.title: OTH: Record revenue and unit growth in 2025, with strong 2026 outlook and expanded financing
feed.summary: Off The Hook YS Inc. (OTH) achieved record revenue of $119.9M (a 21% increase) and sold 426 boats (a 32% increase) in 2025, driven by an expanded broker network, improved inventory, and technology. The company has raised its 2026 revenue guidance to $150–$155M, supported by new financing, the Apex acquisition, and strategic partnerships. This information is based on their Q4 2025 Audio Transcript from March 30, 2026.

feed.title: Murat Kalayoglu to Resign from Cartesian Board, Leaving Science & Technology Committee
feed.summary: Murat Kalayoglu is resigning from Cartesian's Board of Directors, effective March 31, 2026, to pursue other opportunities. He will also cease serving on the Science and Technology Committee and the Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee. Kalayoglu has been on the board since 2023.

feed.title: US appeals court denies bid from families of Boeing 737 Max crash victims to reopen criminal case
feed.summary: A federal appeals court has denied a request from families of the 346 victims of two Boeing 737 Max crashes to reopen a criminal case against Boeing. The families argued that the Department of Justice failed to properly consult them before making a deal with Boeing. The court ruled that the families' rights under the Crime Victims’ Rights Act were not violated, preventing the case from being revived.

feed.title: Will Everest Group’s New Treaty Reinsurance Leader Subtly Shift Its Risk Profile And Strategy (EG)?
feed.summary: Everest Group recently appointed Jason Busti as Head of North America Treaty Reinsurance, a move that strengthens its leadership in a core treaty franchise. This appointment, alongside Jill Beggs' promotion to CEO of Reinsurance, suggests Everest's strategy to balance growing treaty and catastrophic exposure with underwriting discipline. While the appointment doesn't immediately alter the risk narrative, it underscores the company's focus on managing volatility and capital intensity in property catastrophe and treaty reinsurance.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) Trading Up 3.8% - Still a Buy?
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) shares rose 3.8% to $203.43, driven by strong AI demand, planned CPU price increases, and an expanded partnership with Samsung for HBM4 memory. Despite positive catalysts like potential large AI chip orders and bullish analyst ratings, the company faces risks including significant insider selling, a slowdown in gaming revenue, and geopolitical volatility. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus with a price target of $290.53, though institutional positioning remains polarized.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE:JPM) Trading Up 3.7% - Here's Why
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE:JPM) saw its shares trade up 3.7% on Tuesday, driven by strategic initiatives like the "American Dream Initiative" and Mitsubishi's adoption of its Kinexys blockchain, alongside supportive analyst commentary. Despite positive catalysts, the bank faces near-term risks including a legal dispute with Wells Fargo, investor apprehension regarding a CD&R financing deal, and reports of upcoming U.S. layoffs. The company maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with a target price of $337.83, and recently announced a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) Trading Up 1.1% - What's Next?
feed.summary: Pfizer's shares saw a 1.1% increase, trading at $28.08 with slightly below-average volume, amidst positive news regarding its Ibrance data and GLP-1/weight-loss pipeline. While the company reports strong Q4 earnings, it faces significant competition in the GLP-1 market from rivals like Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk. Analyst sentiment is mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $28.19.

feed.title: Labcorp Will Eliminate 83 Positions at Raritan Location in July
feed.summary: Labcorp is set to eliminate 83 jobs at its Raritan, New Jersey site on July 17, as confirmed by a WARN notice. The specific reasons for these cuts and which positions will be affected have not been disclosed by the life sciences firm. These layoffs contribute to a trend of job reductions in the healthcare and diagnostics sectors, potentially impacting Somerset County's economy and prompting local workforce development groups to offer support to affected employees.

feed.title: Embecta Corp. (NASDAQ:EMBC) Sees Large Increase in Short Interest
feed.summary: Embecta Corp. (NASDAQ:EMBC) experienced a 19.4% increase in short interest in March, totaling 5,617,135 shares, which represents about 9.6% of its shares short. Despite this, the company beat its recent quarterly earnings estimates with $0.71 EPS and $261.2M revenue, and issued strong FY2026 guidance. Embecta also offers a quarterly dividend of $0.15, leading to a 6.8% dividend yield, with institutional investors holding approximately 93.8% of its stock.

feed.title: Harvard’s top board adds Blackstone CFO, ex-cabinet secretary
feed.summary: Harvard University has appointed Blackstone CFO Michael Chae and former Health and Human Services Secretary Sylvia Mathews Burwell to its governing board. These appointments come as the university faces increased pressure and scrutiny from the Trump administration, including a lawsuit over alleged antisemitism and disputes regarding federal grant funding. Burwell and Chae will join the 13-member board in July, succeeding departing members.

feed.title: Clean Label Cereals And Weaker Earnings Might Change The Case For Investing In General Mills (GIS)
feed.summary: General Mills recently launched new "clean label" cereals amidst a period of declining sales and net income, and the completion of a large share repurchase program. This article examines how these developments, particularly the focus on reformulated cereals and softer earnings, influence the investment outlook for GIS. Investors are urged to consider the potential for higher overhead and slower profit growth due to these strategic shifts.

feed.title: JKHY Initiated with a 'Buy' Rating by Loop Capital | JKHY Stock News
feed.summary: Loop Capital has initiated coverage on Jack Henry & Associates (JKHY) with a 'Buy' rating and set a price target of $197.00 USD. This follows several other recent upgrades and maintained 'Buy' or 'Equal-Weight' ratings from firms like Wells Fargo, Morgan Stanley, and DA Davidson, with price targets generally increasing. Jack Henry & Associates, a leading provider of core processing and complementary services primarily for small and midsize US banks and credit unions, shows an average analyst target price of $203.09, suggesting a 28.50% upside from its current price.

feed.title: Eastern Bankshares Inc (EBC) Stock Price Quote Today & Current Price Chart
feed.summary: This article provides a stock price quote today for Eastern Bankshares Inc (EBC) along with a current price chart. It promotes online trading through the "Smart Investment App" by Capital.com.

feed.title: New Tenet Healthcare (NYSE: THC) controller to receive $475k salary
feed.summary: Tenet Healthcare announced changes in its accounting leadership, with R. Scott Ramsey retiring as Senior Vice President & Controller and Principal Accounting Officer effective April 30, 2026, and remaining on part-time for transition support. J. Michael Grooms has been appointed as the new Senior Vice President & Controller, effective April 6, 2026, and will become Principal Accounting Officer on May 1, 2026. Grooms' compensation package includes a $475,000 annual base salary, a target cash bonus, and significant equity grants.

feed.title: FibroBiologics completes first CYWC628 batch for clinical trials By Investing.com
feed.summary: FibroBiologics (NASDAQ:FBLG) has successfully manufactured its first batch of CYWC628, a fibroblast-based spheroid therapy for diabetic foot ulcers, intended for upcoming clinical trials. This achievement marks the first large-scale CGMP manufacturing of such a spheroid product and positions the company to initiate Phase 1/2 clinical trials in the first half of 2026. Despite recent operational changes including a reverse stock split, the clinical-stage biotechnology company maintains a strong cash position relative to its debt.

feed.title: Datavault AI and Coppercore Inc. Announce Tokenization of High-Grade Copper Resources into Coppercoin(TM)
feed.summary: Datavault AI and Coppercore Inc. have announced a definitive agreement to tokenize high-grade copper resources into Coppercoin™ digital tokens. This initiative aims to address the projected surge in global copper demand, driven by AI, electrification, and renewable energy, by offering a transparent, fractional, and liquid digital ownership alternative to traditional, complex copper markets. The initial $100 million issuance of Coppercoin™ will leverage Datavault AI's patented blockchain platform, with each token representing five pounds of copper resources, and is set for launch by the end of Q2 this year.

feed.title: Ares Commercial (NYSE: ACRE) extends Morgan Stanley credit facility to 2029 and lifts capacity
feed.summary: Ares Commercial Real Estate Corporation has amended its credit facility with Morgan Stanley, extending the maturity date to July 16, 2029, and increasing the commitment from $250 million to $350 million. An accordion provision allows for a further increase of $50 million, boosting the total potential capacity to $400 million. This move is expected to support the company's stability by providing longer-term and larger secured funding for loan origination and holdings.

feed.title: Purple Biotech (PPBT) CFO details multi-million share options and RSUs
feed.summary: Purple Biotech's CFO, Shai S. Lankry, has updated his equity holdings, revealing significant stock options and restricted stock units (RSUs). The filing shows options to purchase a total of 6,550,000 ordinary shares under the company's 2016 Equity-Based Incentive Plan, with varying exercise prices and expiration dates in 2030. Additionally, Lankry holds 3,254,167 unvested RSUs, which will be settled in ordinary shares based on time-based vesting conditions.

feed.title: Citigroup, PwC Snagged in Clarivate Investor Suit Over Metrics
feed.summary: A federal court has allowed a proposed class action lawsuit against Clarivate Plc, its underwriter Citigroup Global Markets Inc., and auditor PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP to proceed. Investors allege a material misstatement in 2021 public offerings, citing significant changes in key metrics when Clarivate later restated financial figures. The ruling, issued by Judge Eric Komitee in the US District Court for the Eastern District of New York, green-lit the Securities Act claim.

feed.title: Envirotech Vehicles (NASDAQ: EVTV) delays 10-K, posts $38.9M loss
feed.summary: Envirotech Vehicles (EVTV) has delayed its Form 10-K filing for 2025, notifying the SEC it needs more time to complete financial statements and disclosures. The company expects to report a net loss of $38.9 million for 2025, significantly wider than the $8.8 million loss in 2024, despite an increase in total sales from $1.9 million to $5.9 million. These preliminary results are subject to final audit.

feed.title: Delta signs deal with Amazon for faster in-flight Wi-Fi
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines is partnering with Amazon to upgrade its in-flight Wi-Fi services using Amazon's Leo satellite-internet technology. This move aims to provide faster internet for passengers on Delta flights. The airline is noted as Boston's busiest, and the deal signifies a significant enhancement to its passenger experience.

feed.title: Farmmi (NASDAQ: FAMI) CFO submits initial Form 3 insider filing
feed.summary: Farmmi, Inc.'s Chief Financial Officer, Lu Zhimin, has filed an initial Form 3, which is a required statement of beneficial ownership for company insiders. This filing indicates a baseline ownership disclosure with no reported transactions such as purchases, sales, or exercises of shares. The Form 3 clarifies that no insider trading activity is shown, and it serves as an initial inventory sheet for his ownership as CFO.

feed.title: New Jersey American Water Concludes Temporary Treatment Change in Water Treatment Plants Serving Coastal Part of the State – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: New Jersey American Water is concluding its temporary shift from chloramines to chlorine at its Swimming River and Jumping Brook Water Treatment Plants, which serve parts of Monmouth and Ocean Counties. This routine maintenance, which began in mid-February, allowed for system upkeep, and the utility will now resume using chloramines for disinfection, meeting all EPA and NJDEP drinking water standards. Customers may have noticed a slight chlorine taste during the temporary change, which is expected to subside with the return to chloramines.

feed.title: Lululemon convinces US judge to overturn Nike shoe patent verdict
feed.summary: A federal judge in Manhattan has overturned a jury's verdict that Lululemon infringed a Nike shoe patent, invalidating the patent and reversing a $355,450 award against Lululemon. Nike had sued Lululemon in 2023, claiming its shoes infringed two patents related to manufacturing techniques, but the judge ruled the asserted patent was invalid due to obviousness. A separate patent infringement case by Nike against Lululemon concerning its Mirror Home Gym is still ongoing.

feed.title: [Form 4] THERMO FISHER SCIENTIFIC INC. Insider Trading Activity
feed.summary: This article details an insider trading activity by Scott M. Sperling, a director at Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. (TMO). He reported the acquisition of 89.780 Phantom Stock Units, credited to his deferred compensation account at $473.36 per unit, bringing his total holdings to 17,591.800 units. These units are convertible to common stock and will be distributed upon cessation of his board service or a change of control.

feed.title: Darden Restaurants Inc Stock: Steady Growth in Casual Dining Amid Evolving Consumer Trends
feed.summary: Darden Restaurants Inc. (DRI) is experiencing steady growth in the casual dining sector, operating successful brands like Olive Garden and LongHorn Steakhouse. The company's strategic focus on operational efficiency and market expansion, coupled with its robust balance sheet and shareholder returns, positions it well in the competitive U.S. restaurant landscape. Despite potential macroeconomic risks and commodity price volatility, Darden's resilience and multi-brand approach make it an attractive investment for North American portfolios.

feed.title: High‑Yield Autocallable Notes — UBS AG (AMUB) linked to ServiceNow
feed.summary: UBS AG is offering Trigger Autocallable Contingent Yield Notes (AMUB) linked to ServiceNow common stock, with a trade date of March 31, 2026, and maturity on April 2, 2029. These high-yield, high-risk notes offer contingent coupons if ServiceNow's stock closes at or above a specified barrier, and are subject to automatic early call if it closes at or above the initial level. Investors face full downside equity exposure and potential loss of principal if the notes are not called and the final stock level falls below the downside threshold.

feed.title: Brown & Brown sets April 28 investor webcast on Q1 results and AI use
feed.summary: Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE: BRO) will release its first-quarter 2026 earnings on April 27, 2026, after market close. Following this, the company will host an investor update conference call on April 28, 2026, at 8:00 a.m. EDT to discuss Q1 results and how they are leveraging technology, data, and artificial intelligence to deliver innovative solutions. The webcast will be available live on their website, with an archived replay accessible for 14 days.

feed.title: Dycom Industries, Inc. Appoints Company Veteran Bo Gresham as its First Chief Revenue Officer to Lead Next Phase of Growth
feed.summary: Dycom Industries, Inc. has appointed James "Bo" Gresham as its first Chief Revenue Officer, effective March 30, 2026. Gresham, a company veteran with nearly three decades of experience, will oversee strategic market expansion and strengthening stakeholder relationships. His appointment comes as the telecommunications and digital infrastructure industries are experiencing significant growth, driven by fiber deployments and AI integration.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher (NYSE: TMO) director adds phantom stock units grant
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific director Dion J. Weisler was granted 79.2200 phantom stock units on March 28, 2026, as part of his deferred director retainer. These units, valued at $473.36 each, are convertible to common stock on a 1-for-1 basis and increase his total to 2,717.8900 units. The shares will be distributed upon the cessation of his board service or a change of control.

feed.title: Small business training program expands across Permian Basin
feed.summary: The University of Texas Permian Basin and ConocoPhillips are expanding their "Small Biz Builder" program to Monahans, Midland, and Hobbs, New Mexico, after successful pilots in Odessa and Carlsbad. ConocoPhillips provided a $150,000 grant to support the program, which offers hands-on training, expert mentorship, and financial tools to help entrepreneurs build sustainable businesses. The expanded program now includes six in-person sessions tailored for both early-stage and later-stage entrepreneurs, fostering a supportive community among participants.

feed.title: Farmmi (FAMI) director Ruan Hui submits Form 3 insider ownership report
feed.summary: Farmmi, Inc. director Ruan Hui has filed an initial Form 3 insider ownership report, indicating no common stock or derivative holdings at present. This filing is primarily administrative, establishing Hui's insider status without disclosing any buy, sell, or other insider transactions. The report clarifies that no stock transactions or derivative positions are being reported for the director.

feed.title: Executive pay and merger-linked retention at Flushing Financial (NASDAQ: FFIC)
feed.summary: Flushing Financial (FFIC) filed an amended annual report detailing its 2025 executive compensation, board composition, and governance practices, alongside information about its impending all-stock merger with OceanFirst Financial Corp. The report highlights a pay-for-performance compensation structure, robust capital ratios, and retention agreements for key executives linked to the merger. It also discusses the company's strong shareholder returns in 2025 and an 89% approval rate for its executive pay in the advisory Say-On-Pay vote.

feed.title: [PX14A6G] STARBUCKS CORP SEC Filing
feed.summary: This SEC filing (Form PX14A6G) by Starbucks Corporation details an exempt solicitation by the Comptroller of the City of New York on behalf of several New York City pension funds. The solicitation urges Starbucks shareholders to vote against the re-election of Board Directors Jørgen Vig Knudstorp and Beth Ford due to ongoing labor issues and a rollback of independent board oversight since 2021. The filing includes texts of social media posts by the NYC Comptroller disseminating this recommendation.

feed.title: Exxon Mobil prices $169.312M floating-rate notes due 2076
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil (XOM) has signed an underwriting agreement to issue $169.312 million in floating-rate notes due in 2076. These notes are offered under the company's 2014 indenture and supplemental documents, with RBC Capital Markets, J.P. Morgan Securities, and UBS serving as lead managers. The related filings and legal opinions have been incorporated by reference into Exxon Mobil's Form S-3 registration statement.

feed.title: Exxon Mobil (NYSE: XOM) sells $169M floating-rate notes due 2076
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil Corporation has issued $169 million in floating-rate notes due in 2076 through an underwriting agreement with RBC Capital Markets, J.P. Morgan Securities, and UBS Securities. These notes were issued under an existing indenture from 2014, supplemented in 2020, and offered via the company's S-3 shelf Registration Statement filed in February 2026. This financial move, documented in an 8-K filing, serves as a financing transaction for the company.

feed.title: Farmmi (FAMI) director Fu Qinyi files initial Form 3
feed.summary: Farmmi, Inc. director Fu Qinyi has filed an initial statement of beneficial ownership on Form 3, identifying himself as a director of the company. The filing does not report any transactions or derivative positions in Farmmi securities, indicating no trading activity. Form 3 is required for officers, directors, and large shareholders to report their initial ownership upon becoming insiders.

feed.title: Farmmi (FAMI) director Qian Hongdao submits initial insider Form 3
feed.summary: Farmmi, Inc. director Qian Hongdao has filed an initial Form 3 with the SEC, indicating their status as a company insider. This administrative disclosure, required for beneficial ownership, lists no current reportable transactions or derivative positions. The filing primarily serves to establish Qian Hongdao's insider reporting obligations for future trades.

feed.title: Is Nucor Corporation (NUE) One of the Best Stocks to Invest in for Good Returns?
feed.summary: Nucor Corporation (NUE) is considered one of the best stocks for good returns, with KeyBanc initiating coverage with a Sector Weight rating. The company anticipates increased first-quarter earnings, particularly in its steel mills segment due to higher selling prices and volumes. Nucor has also been actively returning value to shareholders through share repurchases and dividend payments.

feed.title: From ground hand to CRO: Dycom promotes company veteran Bo Gresham
feed.summary: Dycom Industries, Inc. has appointed James "Bo" Gresham as its first Chief Revenue Officer, effective March 30, 2026. Gresham, a U.S. Army veteran with nearly 30 years of experience, started at a Dycom subsidiary in 1997 as a ground hand and rose through the ranks before joining corporate leadership in 2023. This promotion signals Dycom's focus on capitalizing on growth in high-speed fiber deployments and AI-driven network infrastructure.

feed.title: Etsy (ETSY) director exercises 41,529 options, holds 59,113 shares
feed.summary: Margaret Mary Smyth, a director at Etsy (ETSY), exercised 41,529 Director Stock Options at an exercise price of $9.67 per share. Following this transaction, her direct ownership in Etsy common stock increased to 59,113 shares. The exercised options had vested in three equal annual installments, subject to her continued service as a director.

feed.title: FG Merger II 2025 10-K: $1.43M Net Income, $0.26 EPS (basic, redeemable)
feed.summary: FG Merger II reported a net income of $1.43 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, driven by $3.04 million in investment income despite an operating loss. The company had no operating revenues but achieved $0.26 basic net income per redeemable share. Its main focus for the period was completing its IPO, funding a trust account, and pursuing a definitive business combination with Boxable.

feed.title: Boxable $3.5B all-stock SPAC merger signed with FG Merger II Corp. (FGMC)
feed.summary: FG Merger II Corp. (FGMC), a SPAC, has signed a definitive merger agreement with Boxable Inc., valuing the modular home builder at $3.5 billion in an all-stock transaction. The merger, which has no minimum cash condition, requires approvals from shareholders, regulatory clearances, and an effective S-4 registration statement. The deadline for completion has been extended to March 31, 2026, and FGMC reported a net income of $1.43 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, primarily from interest on trust funds.

feed.title: First batch of foot-ulcer therapy made for planned human trial
feed.summary: FibroBiologics (FBLG) announced the successful manufacturing of the first batch of its proprietary CYWC628 drug product, intended for upcoming first-in-human clinical trials for diabetic foot ulcers. The product was manufactured under FDA CGMP standards and will be released after safety and quality testing, with a Phase 1/2 trial planned for the first half of 2026. Despite this positive operational news, the company's stock experienced a significant decline, reflecting a negative market reaction.

feed.title: FibroBiologics Announces Successful Manufacturing of CYWC628 Drug Product to Support Upcoming Diabetic Foot Ulcer Clinical Trial
feed.summary: FibroBiologics has successfully completed the manufacturing of its CYWC628 drug product, a fibroblast-based therapy targeting diabetic foot ulcers. This achievement marks the first large-scale CGMP manufacturing of a spheroid product for therapeutic use and will support the company's upcoming Phase 1/2 clinical trial in the first half of 2026. The drug product will now undergo safety and quality testing before release.

feed.title: First Capital 10-K: Net Interest Income $42.15M, EPS $4.89
feed.summary: First Capital (FCAP) reported strong 2025 results with a net interest income of $42.15 million and diluted net income per share of $4.89. The company's net income rose to $16.38 million, driven by higher net interest income and noninterest income, and an improved efficiency ratio. Management highlighted continued focus on credit risk monitoring, operational efficiency, and growth in mortgage origination.

feed.title: Pitney Bowes Launches Temu Integration Within ShipAccel®
feed.summary: Pitney Bowes has launched a new Temu integration within its ShipAccel® solution, aiming to help U.S.-based sellers streamline fulfillment and shipping operations for their Temu marketplace businesses. This integration allows sellers to manage orders, generate shipping labels, and handle fulfillment workflows from a single platform, while also assisting with compliant onboarding to Temu. The initiative supports Pitney Bowes' goal of providing flexible tools for e-commerce sellers in an evolving marketplace.

feed.title: EnerSys (ENS) executive Uplinger adds RSU dividend-equivalent shares to holdings
feed.summary: EnerSys executive Chad C. Uplinger, President Motive Power Global, received multiple small grants of common stock as Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) on March 27, 2026. These RSUs were issued as dividend-equivalent awards tied to a cash dividend paid by EnerSys and relate to unvested RSU awards granted between 2022 and 2025. Following these transactions, Uplinger directly holds 23,753.9318 shares of EnerSys common stock.

feed.title: Rex American Resources 10-K: $650.5M Revenue, $2.50 EPS
feed.summary: Rex American Resources reported fiscal-year net sales of $650.5 million, a 1% increase year-over-year, and basic net income per share of $2.50. The company's profitability was supported by favorable crush spreads and 45Z tax credits, leading to a significant increase in net income attributable to shareholders. Rex American Resources is also planning substantial capital expenditure for expansion and carbon sequestration initiatives, with environmental permits still pending.
